name: judicial_technology_licensing_backlog_oakline_jus_mer
premise: A Judicial Technology response desk is in a fast-moving incident involving judicial technology licensing backlog oakline jus mer. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases harm prevention and accountability. Stakeholders are already framing this as justice vs mercy under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Leila Reed
agents.identity: I am Leila Reed, accountable for a defensible call in Judicial Technology when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays mercy for justice.

agents.name: Luc Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Luc Ishikawa, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Judicial Technology.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so mercy isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of justice.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect mercy.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to mercy to secure justice under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Leila Reed must decide under justice vs mercy. What does Leila Reed do?
